reinvestment_behavior.draft: Salesforce is reinvesting in product development, data and AI capabilities, go-to-market capacity, and platform integration while operating as a profitable SaaS cash generator; the Senior should judge whether reinvestment strengthens retention and expansion rather than diluting margin discipline.
reinvestment_behavior.source_label: CRM FY2025 Form 10-K operating expense and strategy discussion
reinvestment_behavior.excerpt_or_summary: The fixture links SaaS reinvestment and operating leverage to filed revenue, cost, and operating income facts.
reinvestment_behavior.filing_reference: CRM FY2025 Form 10-K, MD&A and EDGAR-derived spine inputs

shareholder_return_behavior.draft: Shareholder returns should be judged against buyback discipline, dilution, stock-based compensation, and acquisition use of capital because SaaS per-share value can be diluted if growth investments do not compound.
shareholder_return_behavior.source_label: CRM FY2025 Form 10-K equity and liquidity discussion
shareholder_return_behavior.excerpt_or_summary: The EDGAR share-count facts and equity disclosures provide the share-base context for per-share value and capital return discipline.
shareholder_return_behavior.filing_reference: CRM FY2025 Form 10-K, liquidity and stockholders' equity disclosures

balance_sheet_discipline.draft: Balance-sheet discipline hinges on preserving flexibility for cloud software competition, acquisition integration, data security incidents, and AI product investment while maintaining the SaaS cash-generator route.
balance_sheet_discipline.source_label: CRM FY2025 EDGAR-derived cash, liabilities, and debt facts
balance_sheet_discipline.excerpt_or_summary: EDGAR-derived cash, debt, and liability concepts frame financial flexibility and WACC inputs.
